meta_title: For Students: SaaS pricing to Solve no understanding of APIs (Beginner Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS pricing and overcome no understanding of APIs. A Beginner-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: no understanding of APIs

content.seo_title: For Students: SaaS Pricing to Solve No Understanding of APIs
content.seo_description: Master SaaS pricing logic to bypass complex API hurdles. Learn to build your first real-world app using Antigravity and Claude Code in this guide.
content.answer_block: If you struggle with APIs, stop building complex integrations first. Start by modeling your SaaS pricing logic locally. By defining your business rules in code before connecting payment gateways, you decouple architectural complexity from commercial logic, allowing you to master data flow before you ever touch a production API endpoint.
content.intro:
You stare at the blank terminal, the weight of a thousand API documentation pages looming over you. The dream of launching your first SaaS feels stalled by the wall of authentication, webhooks, and complex integrations. You aren't failing—you're just starting at the wrong end of the stack.

Professional architecture begins with the business logic, not the external dependencies. By shifting your focus to SaaS pricing structures, you create a sandbox where you control the inputs and outputs. Today, we bridge the gap between 'I don't get APIs' and 'I just deployed a revenue-ready engine' using the precision of Antigravity and the reasoning power of Claude Code.

steps.title: Define Your Pricing Tiers
steps.description: Before touching an API, map out your subscription logic (Free, Pro, Enterprise) to understand how data constraints work.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Python class structure for a SaaS pricing model with three tiers: Free, Pro, and Enterprise. Include methods to check feature access based on user subscription levels.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate pricing_logic.py based on the class structure provided by Antigravity'

steps.title: Simulate User State
steps.description: Create a local mock user object to test how your application responds to different subscription statuses.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a mock User object with properties for 'subscription_status' and 'monthly_requests'. Write a function that validates if a user can access a specific feature.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'test_user_logic.py' --content 'use the mock user object to assert that Pro users get more requests than Free users'

steps.title: Build the Pricing Engine
steps.description: Implement the core logic that calculates billing cycles, allowing you to debug business rules without real payment data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a billing engine that calculates the prorated cost for a user upgrading from Free to Pro mid-month.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'refactor billing_engine.py to include error handling for edge cases in prorated calculations'

steps.title: Abstract API Interaction
steps.description: Create an interface layer that will eventually hold your API keys, keeping your core business logic clean.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design an Abstract Base Class (ABC) for a PaymentGateway. Define standard methods like 'process_payment' and 'cancel_subscription' without implementing the API calls yet.
steps.claude_code_command: claude write 'gateway_interface.py' --content 'define the PaymentGateway ABC and a MockGateway class for local testing'

steps.title: Integrate and Verify
steps.description: Connect your pricing engine to the interface, ensuring your app logic triggers the right actions before hitting a real server.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a main script that ties the PricingEngine to the MockGateway. Demonstrate an upgrade flow where the user status changes upon 'payment' success.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'main.py' --verify 'ensure user status updates correctly when MockGateway.process_payment returns True'

pro_tips: Always build your business logic in a 'Mock' environment; it allows you to test 100% of your failure states without burning API credits.
Use Type Hinting in Python to ensure your pricing tiers don't accept invalid data types, saving hours of debugging later.
Decouple your 'Pricing Engine' from your 'Database Schema' so you can pivot your business model without rewriting your entire backend.
Log every state transition in your pricing model; when you finally connect to a real API, you'll have an audit trail to compare against.
Treat your local mock gateway as a contract; if your code works with the mock, it will work with Stripe or Paddle once you swap the implementation.
content.next_steps: Once your pricing engine is stable, swap your MockGateway for a real integration using a library like Stripe-Python, keeping your existing logic entirely untouched.
content.conversion_block: You now have the foundation of a professional-grade SaaS engine, built with clean architecture and zero API headaches. This is the blueprint that separates hobbyists from founders. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.

Create a local mock user object to test how your application responds to different subscription statuses.
Implement the core logic that calculates billing cycles, allowing you to debug business rules without real payment data.
